title: astragalus eriophyllus subsp. chrysophyllus
intro: Astragalus eriophyllus subsp. chrysophyllus, also known as Desert Milk Vetch, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits associated with Astragalus eriophyllus subsp. chrysophyllus include enhanced immune function, reduced stress and anxiety, and improved cardiovascular health. Astragalus eriophyllus subsp. chrysophyllus is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract for use in herbal remedies.
intro_study: According to "Biological & pharmaceutical bulletin", Astragalus eriophyllus subsp. chrysophyllus may have a role in inhibiting bacterial infection-associated preterm labor by suppressing the productions of IL-6, PGE2, and LTC4 by human amnion cells.
